projectDescription: An AI-powered adaptive traffic signal control system that uses YOLOv8 computer vision to count vehicles at each intersection lane and dynamically assigns green lights to reduce urban congestion.
keyFeatures: Real-time vehicle detection using YOLOv8 (nano, small, or medium variants)
Four-direction intersection zone mapping (North, South, East, West)
Majority-rule signal decision: lane with the most vehicles gets the green light
Pedestrian detection with safety priority override
Emergency vehicle detection via color-based HSV analysis (red/yellow/blue lights)
Live YouTube traffic stream ingestion via yt-dlp and FFmpeg pipe
Local video file support with adjustable playback speed
Web dashboard with real-time camera feed, intersection visualization, and manual controls
REST API for programmatic traffic data querying and signal prediction
Sklearn-based trained model (modelo_semaforo_ia.pkl) for signal phase classification
publicApiSurface: GET / — serves the main web dashboard
GET /video_feed — MJPEG stream of the annotated camera feed
GET /stats — returns current vehicle counts, pedestrians, emergency flag, phase, and priority
POST /predict — accepts manual or live vehicle counts, returns signal prediction (0=Norte,1=Sur,2=Este,3=Oeste)
POST /set_source — switches video source mode: 'youtube' or 'local'
POST /set_speed — sets local video playback speed multiplier
GET /source_status — returns current mode and local video availability
GET /simulate — returns randomized vehicle count data for testing
TrafficCamera(youtube_url, model_path, local_video_path, default_mode, conf, iou, max_det, local_speed)
TrafficCamera.start() — launches the background video processing thread
TrafficCamera.get_frame() — returns the latest JPEG-encoded annotated frame
TrafficCamera.get_counts() — returns the full traffic state dictionary
TrafficCamera.set_phase(phase_id) — manually sets the active signal phase (0-3)
TrafficCamera.set_local_speed(speed) — updates local video playback speed
